footnotes.text: The sense seems to be: "this is not a prodigal expenditure of the precious ointment, but an anointing in preparation for my burial."
Troubled: this emotion is human fear and sadness, occasioned by the impending Passion. St. Thomas calls this scene a brief anticipation of the Agony in the Garden.
They could not believe: faith is a gift of God which often cannot be received because of an obstacle which man puts in its way. The obstacle here is their obstinacy.
Cf. 5, 44. They preferred rather the approval of man than to render due glory to God.
